questions: Employers should be free to lay off workers whenever it improves profitability.
Companies that receive public subsidies should face restrictions on laying off workers.
A company’s primary responsibility is to maximize shareholder value, even if it leads to job losses.
Accepting government support creates an obligation to preserve jobs.
Using layoffs to fund stock buybacks or executive bonuses is ethically acceptable.
Large-scale layoffs primarily benefit the economy by making firms more efficient.
Corporations owe a duty to workers that goes beyond legal minimums.
Restrictions on layoffs generally do more harm than good by interfering with free markets.
It is unfair for companies to lay off workers shortly after receiving taxpayer-funded assistance.
Protecting jobs during downturns leads to healthier companies in the long run.
